children.heading: Fund
children.label: 1 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Acceleration Fund for Research and Development of Homeland Security Technologies established in subsection (c).

children.heading: Homeland security research
children.label: 2 Homeland security research
children.text: The term “homeland security research” means research relevant to the detection of, prevention of, protection against, response to, attribution of, and recovery from homeland security threats, particularly acts of terrorism.

children.heading: HSARPA
children.label: 3 HSARPA
children.text: The term “HSARPA” means the Homeland Security Advanced Research Projects Agency established in subsection (b).

children.heading: Under Secretary
children.label: 4 Under Secretary
children.text: The term “Under Secretary” means the Under Secretary for Science and Technology.

children.heading: Homeland Security Advanced Research Projects Agency
children.label: b Homeland Security Advanced Research Projects Agency

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established the Homeland Security Advanced Research Projects Agency.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 2 Director
children.text: HSARPA shall be headed by a Director, who shall be appointed by the Secretary. The Director shall report to the Under Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: support basic and applied homeland security research to promote revolutionary changes in technologies that would promote homeland security;

children.label: B
children.text: advance the development, testing and evaluation, and deployment of critical homeland security technologies;

children.label: C
children.text: accelerate the prototyping and deployment of technologies that would address homeland security vulnerabilities; and

children.label: D
children.text: conduct research and development for the purpose of advancing technology for the investigation of child exploitation crimes, including child victim identification, trafficking in persons, and child pornography, and for advanced forensics.

children.heading: Targeted competitions
children.label: 4 Targeted competitions
children.text: The Director may solicit proposals to address specific vulnerabilities identified by the Director.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 5 Coordination
children.text: The Director shall ensure that the activities of HSARPA are coordinated with those of other relevant research agencies, and may run projects jointly with other agencies.

children.heading: Personnel
children.label: 6 Personnel
children.text: In hiring personnel for HSARPA, the Secretary shall have the hiring and management authorities described in section 1101 of the Strom Thurmond National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1999 ( note; ). The term of appointments for employees under subsection (c)(1) of that section may not exceed 5 years before the granting of any extension under subsection (c)(2) of that section.15 U.S.C. 3104Public Law 105–261See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Demonstrations
children.label: 7 Demonstrations
children.text: The Director, periodically, shall hold homeland security technology demonstrations to improve contact among technology developers, vendors and acquisition personnel.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established the Acceleration Fund for Research and Development of Homeland Security Technologies, which shall be administered by the Director of HSARPA.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $500,000,000 to the Fund for fiscal year 2003 and such sums as may be necessary thereafter.

children.heading: Coast Guard
children.label: 3 Coast Guard
children.text: Of the funds authorized to be appropriated under paragraph (2), not less than 10 percent of such funds for each fiscal year through fiscal year 2005 shall be authorized only for the Under Secretary, through joint agreement with the Commandant of the Coast Guard, to carry out research and development of improved ports, waterways and coastal security surveillance and perimeter protection capabilities for the purpose of minimizing the possibility that Coast Guard cutters, aircraft, helicopters, and personnel will be diverted from non-homeland security missions to the ports, waterways and coastal security mission.
